OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT IN ITS REPLY RE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 59677066) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MACIAS, JOYCE
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-17-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. BART HAS NOT SATISFIED ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF JOYCE MACIAS IS UNABLE TO PROVE HER SOLE CLAIM FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY. THE ONLY FACTS THAT CAN SERVE TO SATISFY A MOVING PARTY'S SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN ARE THOSE CONTAINED IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT AND SUPPORTED BY EVIDENCE SUBMITTED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. THUS, MS. MACIAS' OBJECTIONS TO THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED IN THE REPLY PAPERS ARE SUSTAINED. BY ITS SEPARATE STATEMENT BART SEEKS TO SATISFY ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN BY RELYING ON MS. MACIAS' DEPOSITION TESTIMONY THAT SHE LACKED INFORMATION ABOUT CERTAIN MATTERS. EXCEPT IN UNUSUAL CASES, AND THIS IS NOT ONE, A MOVING PARTY'S SOLE RELIANCE ON A PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY THAT THE PLAINTIFF LACKED KNOWLEDGE ABOUT PERTINENT MATTERS IS INSUFFICIENT TO SATISFY THE MOVING PARTY'S SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. (WEBER V. JOHN CRANE, INC. (2007) 143 CAL. APP. 4TH 1433). THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Notice Of Defendants Motion To Bifurcate Liability From Damages
Off Calendar Dec-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Bay Area Rapid Transit's motion to bifurcate liability from damages is denied without prejudice to renewing the motion before the trial judge. The trial judge, rather than a law and motion judge, is in the best position to decide whether the trial should be bifurcated. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Stephen J. Gorog, Esq. (415.986.1338, ext.150) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Kevin J. Gray of Harrington Foxx DuBrow & Canter LLP (415.288.6600) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. BART has not satisfied its summary judgment burden of showing that plaintiff Joyce Macias is unable to prove her sole claim for dangerous condition of public property. The only facts that can serve to satisfy a moving party's summary judgment burden are those contained in the separate statement and supported by evidence submitted with the moving papers. Thus, Ms. Macias' objections to the evidence submitted in the reply papers are sustained. By its separate statement BART seeks to satisfy its summary judgment burden by relying on Ms. Macias' deposition testimony that she lacked information about certain matters. Except in unusual cases, and this is not one, a moving party's sole reliance on a plaintiff's deposition testimony that the plaintiff lacked knowledge about pertinent matters is insufficient to satisfy the moving party's summary judgment burden. (Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2007) 143 Cal. App. 4th 1433). The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
